decks.notes: This deck focuses on setting up a lock with Stasis to control the board and limit the opponent's resources. It uses cards like Black Vise and Forsaken City to pressure the opponent while maintaining the Stasis lock. Counterspells and bounce effects like Chain of Vapor are used to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's plays. It also includes cards like Gush and Impulse to dig for key pieces of the combo. The deck aims to stall the game until it can establish the lock and slowly chip away at the opponent's resources.

The main concept of this premodern control deck is to use Oath of Druids to quickly cheat out powerful creatures like Terravore while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Sphere of Resistance and Winter's Grasp. The deck also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Cataclysm to deal with threats, along with mana denial through cards like Rishadan Port and Wasteland. The deck aims to control the game by leveraging efficient threats and disruption, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with its powerful creatures and resource denial.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly play enchantments, particularly enchantresses, in order to draw multiple cards each turn. The deck aims to generate card advantage, mana ramp, and protection through enchantments like Sterling Grove, Wild Growth, and Enchantress's Presence. By utilizing the synergies between enchantments like Opalescence and Parallax Wave, the deck can create powerful combo interactions and overwhelming board presence. The strategy revolves around creating an engine of enchantments that continually fuel each other, ultimately leading to a dominant board state and potential win condition.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, disruptive spells like Sphere of Resistance, and land disruption with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port. The deck aims to ramp up mana quickly with Mox Diamond and utilize the powerful Oath of Druids combo with Terravore to pull big threats from the deck. The inclusion of Sylvan Library helps with card advantage and consistency in drawing into key cards. Overall, the deck seeks to establish control early on and outvalue opponents with its efficient threats and disruption.

The main focus of this deck is to take advantage of the Enchantress theme by utilizing cards like Argothian Enchantress and Enchantress's Presence to draw cards and generate value. The deck aims to set up a board state with multiple enchantments in play, then utilize cards like Opalescence and Parallax Wave to create powerful combos that can control the battlefield or close out the game. The deck also includes cards like Sterling Grove and Seal of Cleansing to protect its enchantments and disrupt opponent's strategies. Overall, the deck is designed to overwhelm opponents with its enchantment synergy and combo potential.

The deck focuses on using efficient creatures and disruption spells to control the board and outvalue the opponent. By utilizing creatures like Basking Rootwalla and Wall of Roots to generate mana, along with powerful spells like Survival of the Fittest and Opposition, the deck can maintain a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of cards like Winter Orb and Tradewind Rider further solidifies its control strategy, while cards like Gilded Drake and Squee, Goblin Nabob add versatility and resilience to the deck's game plan.

The main idea of this deck is to use Tinker to search for Phyrexian Devourer, then use Fling to sacrifice it and deal a huge amount of damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Altar of Dementia, Chromatic Sphere, and Mind Stone to facilitate this combo. Defense Grid and Tsabo's Web help to protect the combo while cards like Tangle Wire slow down the opponent. The deck aims to assemble the combo quickly and efficiently in order to win the game in a burst of damage.

This deck focuses on using artifacts to control the game and disrupt the opponent's resources. By leveraging cards like Goblin Welder, Tinker, and Winter Orb, the deck can manipulate the board state to its advantage. The combination of powerful artifacts like Karn, Silver Golem and Smokestack with disruptive cards like Tangle Wire and Sphere of Resistance creates a lockdown strategy that stifles the opponent's ability to build a board presence or cast spells efficiently. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent by using its control elements to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the en-Kor creatures to create an infinite life combo. By using cards like Daru Spiritualist, Nomads en-Kor, and Shaman en-Kor, you can continuously redirect damage to prevent yourself from losing. This combo is supported by cards like Worthy Cause and Task Force to gain large amounts of life, allowing you to stall the game until you can find a win condition. The deck also includes tools like Eladamri's Call and Living Wish to search for key combo pieces when needed.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with an aggressive swarm of Goblin creatures. By utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to search for key Goblin cards, along with cards like Goblin Warchief and Goblin Piledriver to boost their power, this deck aims to quickly build a threatening board presence. Additionally, cards like Gempalm Incinerator provide removal options for opposing creatures, while cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, this deck is designed to consistently apply pressure and quickly close out games.

This Mono Black Aggro deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruption spells. By utilizing cards like Ravenous Rats and Hypnotic Specter to disrupt the opponent's hand and applying pressure with aggressive creatures such as Nantuko Shade and Bane of the Living, this deck aims to win the game swiftly before the opponent can stabilize. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Cursed Scroll and Wasteland to control the board and prevent the opponent from mounting a defense.

The main concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base and prevent them from playing their threats by using land destruction spells like Stone Rain and Pillage. This allows the player to control the game by keeping the opponent off balance while dealing direct damage with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate. The deck also includes cards like Grim Lavamancer and Rorix Bladewing for additional threats to close out the game quickly. Overall, the strategy revolves around resource denial and efficient removal to outlast the opponent.

The deck revolves around quickly getting cards into the graveyard to set up powerful combo plays with Replenish. Cards like Attunement and Frantic Search help fill the graveyard, while Replenish brings back key enchantments like Opalescence and Parallax Wave to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes control elements like Mana Leak and Decree of Silence to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The strategy focuses on efficiently executing the combo while managing disruption from the opponent to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combo interaction between Replenish and various enchantments in order to overwhelm the opponent with a board full of powerful creatures. Cards like Attunement and Opalescence are used to set up the combo, while cards like Parallax Wave and Decree of Silence protect the board and maintain control. The deck also includes some control elements like Mana Leak and Swords to Plowshares to help navigate through the early game. This deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and win the game quickly through a decisive combo finish.

The deck focuses on using creatures like Birds of Paradise and Wall of Roots to ramp up mana quickly, while using Survival of the Fittest to tutor key creatures like Captain Sisay and Spike Feeder. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and creatures like Spore Frog, while also generating card advantage with Sylvan Library. Gaea's Cradle and Treetop Village provide additional mana sources, allowing the player to quickly deploy threats and outvalue their opponent.

The main idea of this deck is to establish a lock on the game using cards like Stasis, Forsaken City, and Black Vise. By denying your opponent resources and preventing them from untapping their permanents, you can control the game until you can assemble your combo pieces like Brain Freeze or Upheaval to win the game. Counterspells, bounce spells, and card draw are included to help support the combo and disrupt your opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to stall the game until you can combo off for the win.
